contents:
they were expected to present on the “ancient wisdom” they had recovered in only a few weeks with general “consequences” if that wisdom was deemed to not have been truly contained within the Library of Atôs.]
The group traveled with The Lorekeepers to a field outside of the city and set off in an airship. In several weeks they reached The Lorekeeper city of Sanctuary Hills. The city was built in a land of clear streams, two moons illuminating the starry sky. The horizon had a warm glow to it in every direction as during a solar eclipse. The city itself was built like a stronghold on top of a hill. Sturdy walls surrounded the city. Old vines draped themselves over the walls of buildings, amongst many pavilions and triumphant structures. A stone path followed for several miles from the city until it reached a planar barrier, a kind of large shimmering distortion in the air somewhat like a heat wave. Through the barrier the landscape suddenly changed to fields and fields of grain, however the sky was blood red. a [The airship was a strange vehicle. It had a small basket compartment which the entire structure rested on. The basket was attached by a ladder to a large boat like structure with turbines spinning somewhat like an airplane. The vehicle traveled through the elemental plane of air in order to reach The Lorekeeper city of Sanctuary Hills more quickly]
[The journey took about 2 weeks so the party spent time talking to The Lorekeepers. Many of the Lorekeepers had come from rather dubious backgrounds such as planar bounty hunters, exiles, corrupted sorcerers, experiments gone wrong. However others seemed to be following “the path” or believed they could help to restore and preserve the ancient wisdom, others seeked to find wisdom to destroy their enemies, others wanted to reconstruct the library of Atôs. All of The Lorekeepers seemed to have a different interpretation of what kind of knowledge was actually lost, why it should be recovered and what to do with it if it was restored. I recall Amadeo was showing them many of the strange artifacts he had collected in his travels wondering if some of the knowledge of Atôs could be found in the artifacts. Additionally during the journey the party crossed through a planar barrier/portal taking them to The Outer Districts where the Lorekeeper city of Sanctuary Hills was located.]
Gar was taken to one of the halls of discussion to present the wisdom of Atôs that he had recovered. Gar was unable to convince The Lorekeepers that he had indeed recovered ancient knowledge. To provide retribution for wasting The Lorekeepers time by eating the apple beacon and causing them to travel all the way to Altié and back in order to allow Gar to present the wisdom that he had recovered, He was given a choice: He could travel to The Depths to recover umbranol, go to The Orchards to collect more of the golden apples from The Guardian who protected The Orchards, or he could travel through the planar barrier nearby to The Fields and stop the tragedy that was occurring there, as The Fields were beginning to flood with blood.
[The party was particularly upset by the hypocrisy of The Lorekeepers that they decided that the party had not found wisdom of Atôs even though The Lorekeepers really didn't even know what that wisdom was (or if it ever existed at all) and basically all of them had different interpretations of it.]
[In a courtyard outside the many halls of discussion where The Lorekeepers would debate The Library of Atôs was a small model, an artists rendition of what the city of Atôs might have looked like.]
[Umbranol was a thick tar like substance that collected on the souls of those who drowned and were

contents:
Written in Go, Hugo is an open source static site generator available under the Apache Licence 2.0. Hugo supports TOML, YAML and JSON data file types, Markdown and HTML content files and uses shortcodes to add rich content. Other notable features are taxonomies, multilingual mode, image processing, custom output formats, HTML/CSS/JS minification and support for Sass SCSS workflows.
Hugo makes use of a variety of open source projects including:
 https://github.com/yuin/goldmark https://github.com/alecthomas/chroma https://github.com/muesli/smartcrop https://github.com/spf13/cobra https://github.com/spf13/viper  Hugo is ideal for blogs, corporate websites, creative portfolios, online magazines, single page applications or even a website with thousands of pages.
Hugo is for people who want to hand code their own website without worrying about setting up complicated runtimes, dependencies and databases.
Websites built with Hugo are extremelly fast, secure and can be deployed anywhere including, AWS, GitHub Pages, Heroku, Netlify and any other hosting provider.
Learn more and contribute on GitHub.
title: About

contents:
When I Set Off I Was A Troubled Man Twilight Courier
When I set off I was a troubled man
And I only returned with different troubles
I realized the blight of compassion dying
That staying would take my soul
And in preservation of empathy
I left once again, quickly and without regret
